meta_description: RAGEAR is a neurosymbolic recommender system for academic course recommendation that combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic…
body_html:
RAGEAR combines dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information.

Dense retrieval over full lecture transcripts Symbolic Knowledge Graph modelling Courses, lessons, transcript chunks, credits, study plans, and curricular information

Advantages of RAGEAR Over Metadata-Based Approaches

Share of retrieved similarity associated with a course The proportion of relevant transcript chunks retrieved for each course

Rank-based strength of relevant chunks The rank order of relevance of retrieved transcript chunks

Distribution of evidence across lessons The spread of evidence across different lessons within a course

Performance Evaluation of RAGEAR Results show that lecture transcripts improve over metadata-only retrieval, and that RAGEAR further improves ranking quality over a transcript-based normalized SumP baseline.

source.title: RAGEAR: Retrieval-Augmented Graph-Enhanced Academic Recommender
source.summary: Researchers have developed RAGEAR, a novel academic course recommendation system that combines dense retrieval of full lecture transcripts with a symbolic Knowledge Graph to provide more accurate and personalized recommendations. The system uses a graph-aware aggregation function to combine evidence from retrieved transcript chunks to generate course-level recommendations. Evaluations show that RAGEAR outperforms existing approaches, particularly in ranking quality, especially for top-ranked recommendations.
